content:
The official video for “Never Gonna Give You Up” by Rick Astley. 

Never: The Autobiography 📚 OUT NOW! 
Follow this link to get your copy and listen to Rick’s ‘Never’ playlist ❤️ #RickAstleyNever
https://linktr.ee/rickastleynever

“Never Gonna Give You Up” was a global smash on its release in July 1987, topping the charts in 25 countries including Rick’s native UK and the US Billboard Hot 100.  It also won the Brit Award for Best single in 1988. Stock Aitken and Waterman wrote and produced the track which was the lead-off single and lead track from Rick’s debut LP “Whenever You Need Somebody”.  The album was itself a UK number one and would go on to sell over 15 million copies worldwide.

The legendary video was directed by Simon West – who later went on to make Hollywood blockbusters such as Con Air, Lara Croft – Tomb Raider and The Expendables 2.  The video passed the 1bn YouTube views milestone on 28 July 2021.

Subscribe to the official Rick Astley YouTube channel: https://RickAstley.lnk.to/YTSubID

Follow Rick Astley:
Facebook: https://RickAstley.lnk.to/FBFollowID 
Twitter: https://RickAstley.lnk.to/TwitterID 
Instagram: https://RickAstley.lnk.to/InstagramID 
Website: https://RickAstley.lnk.to/storeID 
TikTok: https://RickAstley.lnk.to/TikTokID

Spotify: https://RickAstley.lnk.to/SpotifyID 
Apple Music: https://RickAstley.lnk.to/AppleMusicID 
Amazon Music: https://RickAstley.lnk.to/AmazonMusicID 
Deezer: https://RickAstley.lnk.to/DeezerID

#RickAstley #NeverGonnaGiveYouUp #WheneverYouNeedSomebody #OfficialMusicVideo

import re

# Read the VTT file
with open("yt_video.en.vtt", "r") as f:
    vtt_content = f.read()

# Parse the VTT content to create a clean transcript with timestamps
lines = []
pattern = r'(\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\.\d{3}) --> (\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\.\d{3})\n(.*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)'
matches = re.findall(pattern, vtt_content, re.DOTALL)

for start, end, text in matches:
    cleaned = text.strip().replace('\n', ' ')
    if cleaned.startswith('[♪'):
        lines.append(f"[{start}] ♪ Instrumental ♪")
    elif cleaned.startswith('♪'):
        # Extract lyrics text between ♪ symbols
        lyric_text = cleaned.replace('♪', '').strip()
        lines.append(f"[{start}] ♪ {lyric_text} ♪")
    else:
        lines.append(f"[{start}] {cleaned}")

transcript = "\n".join(lines)

with open("transcript.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write(transcript)

print("Transcript saved!")
print(f"Total lines: {len(lines)}")
PYEOF
"}}

arguments.content:
import re

# Read the VTT file
with open("yt_video.en.vtt", "r") as f:
    vtt_content = f.read()

# Parse the VTT content to create a clean transcript with timestamps
lines = []
pattern = r'(\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\.\d{3}) --> (\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\.\d{3})\n(.*?)(?=\n\n|\Z)'
matches = re.findall(pattern, vtt_content, re.DOTALL)

for start, end, text in matches:
    cleaned = text.strip().replace('\n', ' ')
    if cleaned.startswith('[♪'):
        lines.append(f"[{start}] ♪ Instrumental ♪")
    elif cleaned.startswith('♪'):
        lyric_text = cleaned.replace('♪', '').strip()
        lines.append(f"[{start}] ♪ {lyric_text} ♪")
    else:
        lines.append(f"[{start}] {cleaned}")

transcript = "\n".join(lines)

with open("transcript.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write(transcript)

print(f"Transcript saved! Total lines: {len(lines)}")

{"type": "toolCall", "name": "write_file", "arguments": {"content": "# Video Summary: Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster)

## Metadata

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Title** | Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster) |
| **Channel** | Rick Astley (Official Artist Channel) |
| **Duration** | 3:33 |
| **Upload Date** | October 25, 2009 |
| **Views** | ~1.78 billion |
| **Likes** | ~19.2 million |
| **Song Release** | July 1987 |
| **Album** | *Whenever You Need Somebody* |
| **Writers/Producers** | Stock Aitken and Waterman |
| **Video Director** | Simon West |

---

## Summary

"Never Gonna Give You Up" is the iconic debut single by English singer Rick Astley, released in July 1987. The song was written and produced by the legendary British production trio Stock Aitken and Waterman and served as the lead single from Astley's debut album, *Whenever You Need Somebody*. Upon release, it became a global phenomenon, topping charts in 25 countries including both the UK and the US Billboard Hot 100. In 1988, the track won the Brit Award for Best Single. The album itself reached number one in the UK and went on to sell over 15 million copies worldwide.

The official music video was directed by Simon West — who would later go on to direct major Hollywood blockbusters such as *Con Air*, *Lara Croft – Tomb Raider*, and *The Expendables 2*. The video passed the historic one billion YouTube views milestone on July 28, 2021, cementing its status as one of the most-watched videos on the platform.

Beyond its commercial success, the video gained extraordinary cultural significance through the "Rickrolling" internet meme — a viral prank where users were lured into clicking links expecting one thing, only to be redirected to this very music video. Despite being a sincere pop song about commitment and love, the meme transformed the video into one of the most recognized pieces of internet culture in history. The lyrics promise steadfast devotion: "Never gonna give you up, never gonna let you down, never gonna run around and desert you." This earnest declaration, delivered with Astley's distinctive deep voice against a backdrop of polished 1980s synth-pop production, became an unlikely symbol of online humor and community shared experience across the web.

---

## Key Points

- **Global Chart-Topping Hit**: Topped the charts in 25 countries, including the UK and US Billboard Hot 100, upon its July 1987 release.
- **Brit Award Winner**: Won the Brit Award for Best Single in 1988.
- **Legendary Production**: Written and produced by Stock Aitken and Waterman, the most successful British songwriting/production team of the 1980s.
- **Record-Breaking Album**: Lead single from *Whenever You Need Somebody*, which sold over 15 million copies worldwide and reached #1 in the UK.
- **YouTube Milestone**: Passed 1 billion views on July 28, 2021, becoming one of the first music videos to achieve this feat.
- **Internet Meme Culture**: The video became the centerpiece of "Rickrolling," one of the most famous and enduring internet memes, transforming a sincere pop song into a shared cultural joke.
- **Music Video Legacy**: Directed by Simon West, who later directed major Hollywood films, giving the video lasting production value that still holds up today.
- **Timeless Appeal**: The 4K remaster brings renewed attention to the original 1987 recording, demonstrating the song's enduring popularity across generations.

---

arguments.content:
# Video Summary: Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster)

## Metadata

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Title** | Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster) |
| **Channel** | Rick Astley (Official Artist Channel) |
| **Duration** | 3:33 |
| **Upload Date** | October 25, 2009 |
| **Views** | ~1.78 billion |
| **Likes** | ~19.2 million |
| **Song Release** | July 1987 |
| **Album** | *Whenever You Need Somebody* |
| **Writers/Producers** | Stock Aitken and Waterman |
| **Video Director** | Simon West |

---

## Summary

"Never Gonna Give You Up" is the iconic debut single by English singer Rick Astley, released in July 1987. The song was written and produced by the legendary British production trio Stock Aitken and Waterman and served as the lead single from Astley's debut album, *Whenever You Need Somebody*. Upon release, it became a global phenomenon, topping charts in 25 countries including both the UK and the US Billboard Hot 100. In 1988, the track won the Brit Award for Best Single. The album itself reached number one in the UK and went on to sell over 15 million copies worldwide.

The official music video was directed by Simon West — who would later go on to direct major Hollywood blockbusters such as *Con Air*, *Lara Croft – Tomb Raider*, and *The Expendables 2*. The video passed the historic one billion YouTube views milestone on July 28, 2021, cementing its status as one of the most-watched videos on the platform.

Beyond its commercial success, the video gained extraordinary cultural significance through the "Rickrolling" internet meme — a viral prank where users were lured into clicking links expecting one thing, only to be redirected to this very music video. Despite being a sincere pop song about commitment and love, the meme transformed the video into one of the most recognized pieces of internet culture in history. The lyrics promise steadfast devotion: "Never gonna give you up, never gonna let you down, never gonna run around and desert you." This earnest declaration, delivered with Astley's distinctive deep voice against a backdrop of polished 1980s synth-pop production, became an unlikely symbol of online humor and community shared experience across the web.

---

## Key Points

- **Global Chart-Topping Hit**: Topped the charts in 25 countries, including the UK and US Billboard Hot 100, upon its July 1987 release.
- **Brit Award Winner**: Won the Brit Award for Best Single in 1988.
- **Legendary Production**: Written and produced by Stock Aitken and Waterman, the most successful British songwriting/production team of the 1980s.
- **Record-Breaking Album**: Lead single from *Whenever You Need Somebody*, which sold over 15 million copies worldwide and reached #1 in the UK.
- **YouTube Milestone**: Passed 1 billion views on July 28, 2021, becoming one of the first music videos to achieve this feat.
- **Internet Meme Culture**: The video became the centerpiece of "Rickrolling," one of the most famous and enduring internet memes, transforming a sincere pop song into a shared cultural joke.
- **Music Video Legacy**: Directed by Simon West, who later directed major Hollywood films, giving the video lasting production value that still holds up today.
- **Timeless Appeal**: The 4K remaster brings renewed attention to the original 1987 recording, demonstrating the song's enduring popularity across generations.

---

## Timestamps

| Time | Moment |
|------|--------|
| 0:00 | Introductory instrumental / opening synthesizer sequence begins |
| 0:19 | First verse begins — "We're no strangers to love" |
| 0:43 | First chorus — "Never gonna give you up" |
| 1:01 | Second verse — "We've known each other for so long" |
| 1:25 | Second chorus — "Never gonna give you up" |
| 1:42 | Post-chorus bridge section with backing vocals |
| 1:59 | Instrumental break |
| 2:08 | Synth solo interlude |
| 2:16 | Third verse — repeated lyrics with variation |
| 2:33 | Final chorus build-up with fuller arrangement |
| 3:10 | Extended final chorus |
| 3:25 | Outro — fading refrain and instrumental fade-out |
